2606.03641.title: Gender-Dependent Diagnostic Substitution in LLM Medical Triage: Same Symptoms, Unequal Urgency
2606.03641.paper_summary: We investigate whether large language models produce different medical triage recommendations for identical neurological symptoms when only the patient's stated gender and age vary. Using three model families--Gemini 3.5 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and GPT-5.4-mini--we present a standardized symptom profile (persistent headache, blurred vision, morning nausea, visual disturbances) across seven demographic conditions: three age groups (25, 38, 65) x two genders (male, female), plus a gender-unspecified baseline (n = 30 per condition per model, 630 total trials). We find a stark, systemic gender-dependent triage disparity: young women receive significantly lower emergency room (ER) referral rates than age-matched men (Gemini: 0% vs. 23.3%; Claude: 6.7% vs. 96.7%; GPT: 6.7% vs. 66.7%, all p < 0.001). The disparity disappears at age 65 for all models. The primary mechanism is diagnostic substitution: the models anchor on a gender-associated diagnosis, preferentially classifying young women with Idiopathic Intracranial Hypertension (IIH)--a condition epidemiologically linked to women of childbearing age--while diagnosing men with generic increased intracranial pressure with space-occupying lesions in the differential. This diagnostic closure routes female patients to lower-urgency care (outpatient doctor appointments) despite comparable severity ratings (7-9/10). Our findings demonstrate that clinical LLMs replicate documented human clinical biases by using epidemiological priors to suppress triage urgency, suggesting that AI triage engines must decouple urgency assessment from probabilistic diagnostic priors. We release all code, prompts, and raw results.
2606.03641.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查大型語言模型是否會對相同的神經症狀產生不同的醫療分診建議，當僅有病人的性別和年齡變化時。使用三個模型系列——Gemini 3.5 Flash、Claude Sonnet 4.6 和 GPT-5.4-mini——我們在七個人口統計條件下呈現一個標準化的症狀檔案（持續性頭痛、視力模糊、早晨噁心、視覺干擾）：三個年齡組（25、38、65歲）x 兩個性別（男性、女性），加上一個性別未指定的基線（每個條件每個模型 n = 30，共630次試驗）。我們發現明顯的系統性性別依賴性分診差異：年輕女性的急診室（ER）轉診率顯著低於年齡相符的男性（Gemini: 0% vs. 23.3%；Claude: 6.7% vs. 96.7%；GPT: 6.7% vs. 66.7%，均 p < 0.001）。對於所有模型，這種差異在65歲時消失。主要機制是診斷替代：模型基於性別相關的診斷，優先將年輕女性歸類為特發性顱內高壓（IIH）——這是一種流行病學上與育齡女性相關的病症——而將男性診斷為具有占位性病變的普通顱內壓增高。這種診斷封閉將女性患者引導至低緊急性護理（門診醫生預約），儘管嚴重程度評分相當（7-9/10）。我們的研究結果顯示，臨床大型語言模型通過使用流行病學先驗來抑制分診緊急性，複製了已記錄的人類臨床偏見，這表明 AI 分診引擎必須將緊急性評估與概率診斷先驗解耦。我們釋放所有代碼、提示和原始結果。
2606.03641.author: Qi Han Wong et.al.
